title: Wrong bind address in docstring: says 127.0.0.1:6000 but service listens on all interfaces (:6000)
suggested_labels: good first issue, Type: Documentation, Type: Bug
summary: JujuIntrospect.install's docstring (src/jujuintrospect.py:22) states 'The systemd unit will start juju-introspect listening on 127.0.0.1:6000'. The actual unit file src/configs/juju-introspect.service:9 runs `ExecStart=/usr/bin/juju-introspect --listen=:6000`, i.e. it binds to ALL interfaces (0.0.0.0:6000), not loopback. The docstring is factually wrong and is also self-contradictory with the charm's purpose: it calls `open-port 6000/TCP` (src/charm.py:52) and advertises a Parca scrape target at *:6000 (src/charm.py:30) for REMOTE profiling, which only works because the service is not bound to 127.0.0.1.
why_good_first_issue: One-line docstring correction with an unambiguous source of truth (the .service file). No behaviour change, no test change. Teaches a newcomer to cross-check docstrings against the actual systemd ExecStart and to understand `--listen=:PORT` vs `--listen=127.0.0.1:PORT`. Verifiable purely by reading two files.
repro_steps: grep -n 'listen\|127.0.0.1\|6000' src/jujuintrospect.py src/configs/juju-introspect.service. Observe docstring at src/jujuintrospect.py:22 claims 127.0.0.1:6000 while src/configs/juju-introspect.service:9 uses `--listen=:6000` (empty host => all interfaces).
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD. src/jujuintrospect.py:22 = 'The systemd unit will start juju-introspect listening on 127.0.0.1:6000'. src/configs/juju-introspect.service:9 = 'ExecStart=/usr/bin/juju-introspect --listen=:6000'. `--listen=:6000` binds to 0.0.0.0, contradicting the docstring. README.md:5 and charmcraft.yaml:6 only say 'port 6000' (no host), so they are fine; only the code docstring is wrong. Unit suite green (5 passed, tox -e unit), unaffected since this is docs-only.
fix_hint: Change src/jujuintrospect.py:22 to say the unit listens on port 6000 on all interfaces (e.g. '...listening on :6000 (all interfaces)') to match --listen=:6000. Do NOT 'fix' it the other way by adding 127.0.0.1 to the unit file -- that would break remote scraping, which is the charm's purpose.
notes: Machine/systemd charm; 0 open issues -> self-identified. ops via ops.main. Unit env builds and passes (5 tests; charm.py 100% covered). Note the .service file also has trailing whitespace on most lines (cosmetic) if a contributor wants a tiny extra cleanup.

title: install() copies the systemd unit file but never calls daemon_reload()
summary: JujuIntrospect.install (src/jujuintrospect.py:23-24) does shutil.copy('src/configs/juju-introspect.service', self.SVC_PATH) to write a new systemd unit file, but never runs `systemd daemon-reload` afterwards. systemd only picks up new/changed unit files after a daemon-reload; relying on the subsequent service_resume (start, src/jujuintrospect.py:34) to notice the unit is fragile and version-dependent. The operator_libs_linux v1 systemd lib already provides daemon_reload() (lib/charms/operator_libs_linux/v1/systemd.py:279), so the fix is small. remove() (src/jujuintrospect.py:27-30) similarly deletes the unit file without a daemon-reload.
why_good_first_issue: Localised fix in a tiny helper class: import is already present (from charms.operator_libs_linux.v1 import systemd) and the needed function exists. Add systemd.daemon_reload() after the copy in install() (and optionally after the remove in remove()). It's a well-known charm best practice for managing systemd units, so it is a good slightly-above-trivial first issue. A matching unit test can assert daemon_reload is called.
affected_files: src/jujuintrospect.py:23-24 (install: shutil.copy with no daemon_reload); src/jujuintrospect.py:27-30 (remove: os.remove with no daemon_reload); helper available at lib/charms/operator_libs_linux/v1/systemd.py:279 (daemon_reload)
repro_steps: grep -rn 'daemon_reload' src/ -> no matches (the charm never reloads systemd). grep -n 'def daemon_reload' lib/charms/operator_libs_linux/v1/systemd.py -> exists at line 279. Inspect src/jujuintrospect.py:install() -> copy then nothing. Baseline: export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit.
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD. `grep -rn daemon_reload src/` returns nothing, so the charm writes/removes /etc/systemd/system/juju-introspect.service (src/jujuintrospect.py:16,23-24,27-30) without ever calling daemon-reload, while daemon_reload() is available (systemd.py:279). Unit coverage marks src/jujuintrospect.py:24 (install body) as Missing, i.e. only exercised at functional/integration tier; unit baseline 5 passed (tox -e unit), charm.py 100%, jujuintrospect.py 52%.
fix_hint: In install(), after shutil.copy(...), call systemd.daemon_reload(). Consider also calling it in remove() after os.remove(self.SVC_PATH). Add/extend a unit test that patches charms.operator_libs_linux.v1.systemd and asserts daemon_reload() is invoked. (The functional test in tests/functional can validate the real reload.)
notes: Machine/systemd charm; 0 open issues -> self-identified. Slightly higher difficulty than the docstring fix because it touches behaviour and benefits from a test, hence 'medium'. Real correctness concern: without daemon-reload, a changed unit file may not be honoured on upgrade. Flagged as unit-tier because the missing call is provable in code + a unit test can cover the fix; full end-to-end validation is the functional/LXD env (tox -e functional), which this run did not execute.
